---
title: "Leo quickstart"
subtitle: "From zero to your first deployed program in under 30 minutes."
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status:** Stub. Full chapter coming soon.
:::

## The question

You've read the theory. Now you want to actually deploy something. What's
the shortest path from a fresh laptop to a Leo program live on Aleo testnet?

## What this chapter will cover

- Install: `cargo install leo-lang --locked` (Leo v4.0.x — pinned)
- `leo new my_program` and the project layout
- Your `.env`: NETWORK, ENDPOINT, PRIVATE_KEY (testnet only — never paste mainnet)
- The faucet: <https://faucet.provable.com/>
- `leo build` → `leo run` → `leo test` → `leo deploy`
- The two flags that trip up everyone: `--broadcast` (required!) and `--priority-fees` (plural!)
- Verifying deployment on aleoscan.io

## Interactive viz planned

A "first program" trace: paste Leo source, see it tokenize → parse → typecheck
→ compile to AVM. (Possibly via WASM-compiled Leo running in the browser.)

## Why this is short

Most Leo "getting started" guides bury you in cryptographic preamble. This
one assumes you read the previous 10 chapters and just want to type the
right commands.

## Companion: aleo-skills

If you have Claude Code installed, just run:

```
/plugin install casualcomputer/aleo-skills
```

The plugin's always-on rules (`CLAUDE.md`) include the Leo CLI gotchas
(`--broadcast`, `--priority-fees`, default to testnet, etc.) so you'll
catch the common mistakes automatically.
